output: T15 completed. `reigh-app/CLAUDE.md` is a symlink to `.cursorrules`, so I appended the new guidance there. The doc now states that required-provider hooks must throw when context is missing, that `Safe` variants are opt-in only for genuinely optional consumers with inline justification, and it cites the motivating incident where `EMPTY_FORM` no-op setters caused the media-lightbox edit textarea to silently drop typing. Verification confirmed the new section is present and no new `.md` files were created. I also checkpointed the batch result in `execution_batch_12.json`.
commands_run: git ls-files --stage -- CLAUDE.md && git check-ignore -v CLAUDE.md || true
readlink CLAUDE.md && ls -l CLAUDE.md && git diff --raw -- CLAUDE.md

task_updates.executor_notes: Appended the required context-hook guidance to `reigh-app/CLAUDE.md` via its `.cursorrules` symlink target. The new section explicitly requires missing-provider hooks to throw, limits `Safe` variants to genuinely optional consumers with inline justification, and references the media-lightbox `EMPTY_FORM` no-op setter incident as the reason. Verification confirmed the section is present and no new `.md` files were created.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. `reigh-app/CLAUDE.md` now documents the no-op-default-context antipattern: required-provider hooks must throw, `Safe` variants are opt-in only for genuinely optional consumers with inline justification, and the note cites the `EMPTY_FORM` no-op setter bug that dropped typing in the edit textarea. No new `.md` files were created.
